acceptance_targets: [CH13_DIALOGUE_CALIBRATION_LEAK] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:203 uses a different remembered event than the Grenn's-barn/cow example, the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:235 no longer hinges on the style-bible tomato phrasing, and Maren's answer at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 communicates why he remembers without turning into a quotable thesis about himself. Fail if the emotional peak still depends on those recognizable calibration scaffolds or on a polished explanatory line.

[CH13_CRAFT_PREDECODED_BETRAYAL] Pass if, reading from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:29 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:115, the reader can infer Derry's impending departure from staging, silence, and Derry's halting logistics talk without the chapter separately labeling him as rehearsing, bridge-building, and already dealt. Pass if the post-embrace beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227 adds fresh friction or is removed. Fail if the revised scene still re-explains the same subtext in multiple diagnostic summaries.

[CH13_PROSE_APHORISTIC_ENDING] Pass if the revised ending still shows the empty campsite, the iron pot, Caul's operational advantage, and Maren choosing the left fork, but the closing paragraphs after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 no longer explain friendship, betrayal, or loneliness in abstract aphoristic formulations. Fail if the revised close still contains equivalent summary statements where the scene has already made the point through the pot, fork, and altered travel conditions.

[OVERSTATEMENT_CH13] Pass if 3483 communicates Derry's absence in two sentences or fewer without cataloging what's missing, and if 3497 handles the pot without converting it to explicit thematic statement about the friendship's arc. Fail if the passage still extends the departure's emotional content beyond what the scene details already deliver.
[C-03] Ch 13's reference to Pell's age/birthday matches the canonical age established across the manuscript.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:203 uses a different remembered event than the Grenn's-barn/cow example, the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:235 no longer hinges on the style-bible tomato phrasing, and Maren's answer at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 communicates why he remembers without turning into a quotable thesis about himself. Fail if the emotional peak still depends on those recognizable calibration scaffolds or on a polished explanatory line.
findings.problem: The farewell exchange leaks calibration language from the style bible and then resolves on a polished self-explanatory line, so the chapter's emotional centerpiece reads workshopped rather than overheard. Derry's shared-memory beat at line 203 closely tracks the forbidden example-line scaffold about Grenn's barn and the cow, the garden callback at line 235 lands on the same tomatoes phrasing seeded in the style bible, and Maren's reply at line 239 turns into a neat thesis about his own profession instead of pressured speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the memory beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:203 with a different shared incident that belongs specifically to this draft, and rebuild the garden callback at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:235 around a fresh concrete detail rather than the tomato-example scaffold. Revise Maren's answer at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 so it stays emotionally legible but sounds like Maren under strain, not an authored explanation of his habits.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, reading from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:29 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:115, the reader can infer Derry's impending departure from staging, silence, and Derry's halting logistics talk without the chapter separately labeling him as rehearsing, bridge-building, and already dealt. Pass if the post-embrace beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227 adds fresh friction or is removed. Fail if the revised scene still re-explains the same subtext in multiple diagnostic summaries.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps decoding Derry's withdrawal for the reader instead of trusting the staged evidence. Silence, posture, failed humor, and Derry's own circling speech already establish the pending departure, but the narration and internal voices keep rephrasing that same fact as rehearsal, bridge-building, already-made-deal, and absolution. The result is a scene whose pressure is repeatedly explained instead of allowed to tighten.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thin the interpretive gloss around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:77, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:131 so the body-language engine carries more of the scene. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227, either cut Aldric's motive-summary or replace it with a line that creates new pressure rather than translating what the exchange has already made legible.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending still shows the empty campsite, the iron pot, Caul's operational advantage, and Maren choosing the left fork, but the closing paragraphs after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 no longer explain friendship, betrayal, or loneliness in abstract aphoristic formulations. Fail if the revised close still contains equivalent summary statements where the scene has already made the point through the pot, fork, and altered travel conditions.
findings.problem: After the chapter has already landed Derry's departure through the empty camp and the left-behind pot, the closing prose swerves into abstract, aphoristic paraphrase. Lines about what friendship is, what betrayals-from-love are, and the 'specific, non-transferable loneliness' of the moment step outside Maren's colloquial close-third and start summarizing the chapter's meaning instead of letting the pot, fork, road, and changed weight of the pack carry it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the concrete arrival point at Derry's absence and the pot, then compress and re-ground the ending around observable consequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:229, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:285, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:289. Preserve the must-land beats that Derry is gone, Caul has the direction, and Maren takes the left fork toward the Crook, but express the emotional cost through body, object, motion, and tactical reality rather than thesis language.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if 3483 communicates Derry's absence in two sentences or fewer without cataloging what's missing, and if 3497 handles the pot without converting it to explicit thematic statement about the friendship's arc. Fail if the passage still extends the departure's emotional content beyond what the scene details already deliver.

evidence.excerpt: 3483: He could feel the place where Derry had been like a gap in the air. Not dramatic. Not a wound. Just the specific absence of a particular person — a voice, a warmth, the sound of nervous chatter filling every silence because silence was the one thing Derry couldn't tolerate. Nineteen days of that chatter, and before that eleven years of it, and before that the two of them at seventeen in a dead man's barn with a cow named Patience and the beginning of a fraud that would feed both their families and ruin more than either of them could count.

3497: Two things in his pack that used to be fake and weren't anymore. The bowl and whatever was left of the friendship, which had been built on a lie and had turned into something genuine and was now sitting at the bottom of his bag in the shape of an iron pot that a man polished every night because it was the thing he could care for when his daughter was two hundred miles away.

He put it in his pack, on top of his ceramic eating bowl — the bowl he'd bought at seventeen to fake rituals with, the bowl that had become something real somewhere between the first botched consumption and the awful half-success by the creek bed when his body had tried to turn itself inside out and Vess had talked him through breathing like a woman coaching a stablehand through a broken leg.

findings.problem: The morning after Derry's departure, two passages over-explain turns the scene has already earned through action. At 3483, the image of Derry's absence ('a gap in the air') is strong, but the passage extends into a 7-line biographical inventory of what's missing ('a voice, a warmth, the sound of nervous chatter... Nineteen days of that chatter, and before that eleven years of it...'). The preceding paragraph's concrete details — folded blanket, swept ground, the iron pot left by cold ashes — already communicate the absence with more specificity and force than the abstract catalog. At 3497, the pot's symbolic weight is converted to explicit thesis: 'Two things in his pack that used to be fake and weren't anymore... whatever was left of the friendship, which had been built on a lie and had turned into something genuine.' The reader understands the pot's weight from watching Derry polish it nightly; the thesis sentence translates image into argument.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3483-3484 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3495-3498. At 3483, keep 'the specific absence of a particular person' and cut the biographical inventory that follows. The folded blanket and iron pot (3479) are doing the work; the abstract loss-language weakens their effect. At 3497, compress or cut the sentence that interprets the pot as a thesis about friendship transformation. Option: let the pot detail sit as action (Maren puts it in his pack on top of the bowl) without the narrator articulating what the juxtaposition means.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13's reference to Pell's age/birthday matches the canonical age established across the manuscript.
findings.evidence: Ch 3 L577: 'she was four'; Ch 13 L3345: 'She turned two while we were on the road. I wasn't there.' If the canonical age is approximately 3 (per Ch 7/10 references), then 'turned two' in Ch 13 is a distinct error from the 'four' error in Ch 3.
findings.problem: Ch 13 L3345 has Derry saying Pell 'turned two while we were on the road.' This contradicts not only Ch 3 (where she is 'four') but also Ch 7 and Ch 10 (where she is 'three' or 'going to be three'). A child who is 'going to be three next month' in Ch 10 cannot have 'turned two' in Ch 13, which takes place only days later. This appears to be a separate error from the Ch 3 age reference.
findings.rewrite_direction: Correct L3345 to match the canonical age established in C-01. If Pell is turning 3, revise to: 'She's turning three while we're on the road. I won't be there.' This preserves the emotional beat (missing the birthday) while fixing the numerical error.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
